description: My first and best pride parade, made out with a girl in public for the first time and it was g o r g e o u s

While living  away with friends this is where I started walking around publicly as trans, although I didn't understand people comments because my hebrew was terrible at the time, it just made it easier to ignore and become more confident
First gay sex, it was awsome.
At my first IGY seminar, I felt so fully enveloped by queer love that I knew I would be okay. I found my new family.
My first kiss with another girl. In the middle of the street, with people walking by. I was so nervous. I didn't know what to expect.

We crowd in all of us/
the ingathering of exiles/
Stomping the ground of this/
tiny space of wholeness/
we have carved ourselves/
with carresses, and kisses set to blaring synths/

In this city/ 
joy is light/ 
caught in the cave-like walls/ 
of a carved-out gay bar.

Forever my gay bar. Sharing a piece of joy with 15 lifelong friends and friends for the night.

After talking about brotherhood between Jews and Muslims, we had sex in park next to the King David Hotel. As soon as he came, he tried to rob me. But it seemed so half-hearted that I knew he was just trying to save face. I took his hand off my collar and walked away.
Came out to my friend while hiking and camping here on a gap year. I was excited and terrified and almost tripped over some rocks.
FREE PALESTINE
I kissed a soldier here.
I gave my first rimjob here. After that I fall for this guy.
Someone on my youth trip told me I was the first bi person she ever met. Thats when I knew I didn't belong on this trip.

here, we kissed for the first time, and it was the best and sexiest kiss of my life. i hated this city, but when we left the bar hand-in-hand, i thought: here i am, with a perfect woman in the holy city of G-d and the people and the night is beautiful.
“Transparent” helped me to understand myself

I was forced to come out to aggressive border control agents here. They denied my sexuality and were overtly homophobic. As a queer Arab woman, they saw me as a threat. The idea of Tel Aviv being a haven for LGBT+ people in the Middle East is a myth. I found greater acceptance in Palestine. Long live Palestine!
Here in the waves of Hilton Beach, my friend and I met an extraordinary man who became a very close friend who enriched our lives and broadened our horizons of sexual experiences.
my brother took his own life here february 8, 2018. i will never know what he truly felt but he came out as gay in the note he left. i miss him more than words can explain.<3 יהודה, אני אוהב אותך
I'm not sure why but I sometimes think of that night we stayed up talking
The silliest New Years Eve, nothing like we wanted it to be, but I think I fell in love with everyone a little bit that night
After a beautiful dinner, we stood together looking out to sea and he touched my hand. In that perfect, simple gesture, I knew what we had was special.
For a year, I lived here in silence and thought I could ignore this part of myself. I thought I could silence it. I was wrong. It was a great experience otherwise though
I wanted to sit next to you on the bus and listen to music together. We were friends, then you cut me off. Still think about you. Hope you're doing okay

I wanted to go to a gay bar. Instead I went to a bar with you and we did araq shots. Afterwards, I came out to you when explaining why I couldn't move here. I trusted you and barely knew you. Hope you're happy you stayed
Came out to and made my first gay friend here! Love her a lot :)
Came out to my first gay friend here.  Love her a lot!
We hooked up in your parent's house. I haven't been able to replicate the silence during sex again. Fear helps.
I came out as bi for the first time here. It felt so liberating.
I told her I loved her
came out to a friends and a rabbi here.

spent a lot of time telling a girl i loved her 
wish i could go back to then
We came out to each other here and started an LGBT underground on the other side of the world.

I watched an older ultra-orthodox woman accidently stumble into a Lesbian Bar and order 2 drinks before realizing where she was.  She realized, shrugged pulled the bartender into a kiss, said "Who am I to judge?", and left the bar.
The founding forefathers of pinkwashing
You were so terrible to me here. No one should ever have treated me this way. Especially a woman I loved so deeply. I don't think I can ever forgive you.

My first sex ever, at age 19. It was only oral, but we were both scared and uncertain. It was quick, and annonymous, I don't even know your name and will never recognize if I saw you now, 10 years later.

Here I realized for the first time I might not be straight. We were doing a school play, and all of a sudden realized I am really interested in looking more at you, while we were in our underwear, changing.
A BIG gay crush happened here
had sex on a roof in the rain on my birthday
curly brown dyed blonde, your hair was so beautiful
I danced shirtless in a waterfall. It was the first time I've felt comfortable being shirtless in public since having top surgery over two years ago.
My first pride parade. 30 of my friends and I dying from heatstroke, singing out hearts out.
My first ever pride march for the first time ever got to walk around wearing rainbows first time ever feeling that safe and accepted
First gay pride march I went the safest I’ve ever felt I was covered in rainbows and grinning ear to ear
realized i'm trans.

here we talked for hours while you were working at the ice cream factory. it was a sweet summer love, and though nothing much came out of it, i'm glad it happened. it affirmed my gayness, and i think yours too.

in the 2017 tel aviv pride, my first pride ever, i felt for once like i fit in. these were my people. i felt like nobody cared about my sexuality or gender - i felt like i was loved by 100k strangers.

in the 2017 tel aviv pride, my first pride ever, i felt for once like i fit in. these were my people. i felt like nobody cared about my sexuality or gender - i felt like i was loved by 100k strangers.
You were the first woman I slept with... I don't know if I told you that. I wish you had been more careful with my trust.

I came out to my parents through a letter the day after my 18th birthday and then slept at friends' houses for a week because I was terrified. When I went back and my dad made a lighthearted pun about my orientation I almost cried with relief.
Wore my first tie
Came out to my friends
Wore my first Tie
a girl kissed me for the first time
My first date with my first girlfriend was in a hidden park here. It was sweet and special and I'll always look back on it fondly.
This is where I realized, without a doubt, that I'm bi. Shout-out to the cute delivery girl who I stammered at with hearts in my eyes.
Came out as bi for the first time in public to another bi friend. It was so liberating. We sat on the grass on talked for a hour.
Came out as bi for the first time in public to a good friend. Talked with a whole hour and it was really liberating
lost my virginity here at the ripe old age of 23. got so out of breath i thought i was going to pass out. she made me dinner afterwards. we're not together anymore but i have no regrets.
Pretty sure I fell in love a little for the first time drinking leftover beer in the rain on the roof of the hostel.
My first hookup
Spent new years' eve watching a drag show with a gorgeous femme woman who bought me shots of Araq for the first time and introduced me to her friends.

A friend from the internet who lives and studies in Jerusalem hosted myself and my friend/partner as we were traveling through Israel and Palestine. We spent 3 days in her apartment hanging out with other LGBT people and leftists in general and having lots of weed, wine, and amazing conversation. She took us out almost every night and showed us the best parts of the city. Our last night in town, we ended up having a threesome. It felt like we were living in a magical little gay party bubble. We had barely known each other for a week even, but we already related to and trusted each other so much. We are all good friends now, and share our privately kept memories of the holy city.
Kissed my best friend here
Was an a birthright trip here when I first kissed my now girlfriend. It was drunk and sloppy and started as a Juul pass, but it was beautiful and felt so right. I love you, OOT.
hey nat I love you
GUESS WHO'S GAAAYYYYYYYYY AS FUCK

We matched on tinder and you invited me for tea. I ended up staying for 3 nights in a row. We talked about religion, sex, being circumcised, mental health, love and watching the same kids tv shows growing up event though we were from two very different countries. I left for Tel Aviv and you came after me. I still think about you everyday, I hope you're not mad at me.
A gay person exists here and annoys her roommates
Visited my first gay bar after living in a homophobic country
Had some wonderful sex with women here. The admiration of a blonde and experienced women made me live.
Had some wonderful sex with women here. The admiration of a blonde and experienced women made me live.
Long live the Bastet!
Came out as bi to a friend, sat on the grass for a whole hour and talked,

I met my first real-life queer friends here, had my first girlfriend, got dumped by my first girlfriend, sat discussing and explaining queer theory with/to younger classmen. I saw so many queer people thriving at this school. maybe it wasn't perfect (in fact I have many bad memories from here), but there are so few places I would've felt comfortable coming out at so early in life.
I told the guy the guy who told me about bisexuality that I thought I might be bi. despite being totally accepting of his bi sister, he said he thought I was confused.
my mother finally started accepting my sexuality after she drove me to visit her gynecologist

I was in middle school and thought I was straight. Then I accidentally kissed a girl on the cheek. Yes, accidentally. In front of a teacher. And then I ran away in panic to contemplate the fact that maybe I’m not as straight as I though.
I came out for the first time. Also confessed my feelings to the guy who later became my boyfriend for about a year. We sang duets from musicals and read each other poetry. We were nerds.

You came to drink tea in these Turkish tea glasses. At first, I didn't think anything is too special, just regular sex with a tourist. But we felt things for the next days until you had to return to your boyfriend. I hope you're just fine, I wish possibilities were different.
Met some queer Israeli activists here. They really inspired me.

i visited at the western wall with my friends for the first time since coming out as transmasc. I was terrified they would throw me out if they realized i was born female. my heart was ready to burst when i followed my friend onto the men's side. when i finally reached the wall without incident i touched it and thanked God for letting me be who i truly was.
you told me not to lick the salt crystals so i ate an entire one just to prove to you that my queer ass could do anything i put my mind to
I accidentally kissed you on the cheek then ran away. That was my first clue that I might not be as straight as I thought.

I moved here a few months ago with my queerplatonic, the man who I love platonicly and openly and is also my spouse. Our families are tough on us but we will make it better. For the first time in my life, I feel good about myself and my life. love you hunny <3
my shitty high school where i first discovered i was a lesbian. kinda homophobic so i was scared to come out
where i first met her. she's my best friend but i kinda had a crush on her to be honest
where my first IGY group would meet. you guys made me feel welcome. i love you dearly
had a crush on you
I am so lost.
Saw my first pride parade here while on holiday. I was seven and I thought the colours looks so fun
Went to pride after the organizers for my Birthright trip fought to allow our group to go on behalf of me and the other couple queer kids there. Mishpacha
I turn the cats queer.
Bby it ain’t no lie I’m bi bi bi
came here on a birthright trip. Became instant friends with this girl who im still friends with 4 years later. she was the first girl i had real feeling for and made me realize i wasn't straight.

At the Ulpena on the bench in front of the far building, came out to a friend as bi...who subsequently came out as bi, too. We don't talk anymore but that was my first face-too-face coming out. I'll never forget it.

My first pride march
I have never felt safer and more camfortble in my own skin beafore first time covered in rainbows smiling ear to ear
I met you here for the first time, those two weeks in the summer when we were both awkward tweens. It's been 3 years, I'm happy that we're together now

I finally realised that there would be people in this world that I could fall in love with, that I could be happy with. That I could live a religious life and still love women in a way so many deem unholy. I knew you for ten days and still I think of you and your righteous eyes.

This is where I fell in love with you for the first time, you with your coiled hair and bright brown eyes. The smartest person I'll ever meet, and for a very long time, the girl I thought I was destined to love forever.
Had a couple of sweet crushes on lovely humans here. Helped keep me grounded.
aro-ace. went to scool here & had a lot of friends like me. we all knew who we are from a very young age. because of this environment i wasn't in the closet for even a day. didn't have to.
Saw a pride flag in Israel for the first time
Danced in the street with her.
Shortest day of the year, when it rains and everything is blurry with fog.
First time I came out to anyone face-to-face was on the picnic bench outside of the farthest building. She came out to me right after. You'll get through yeshiva or ulpena, and you're not alone.
as soon as i stepped out of class i saw u waiting for me and i wanted to kiss u but never told you about it and buried the gay away
My first pride parade ever. Among the crowd, I finally felt like I belonged.
Every year there is a seminar here for the LGBTQ student's association here. I've never felt so at peace.

Had my first crush on a girl. I kept trying to find reasons to hold her hand, but she always beat me to it. One time on a bus she fell asleep with her arm around my waist and her head on my shoulder. We confeesed to each other but in the end nothing came out of it and we drifted apart. I wish I knew how to love when I was 16 years old. I still feel sorry.
It was somewhere around here - my first IGY experience. Met one of my best friends there, and it really helped me come out of the closet in my personal life, too.
I fell in love with my best (same sex) friend here. It was the worst heartbreak of my life.
We kissed here for the first time. It was so corny.
We were making out at the pride parade and nearly had sex right on that bench
Sex in the bathroom. Pretty sure half of the lgbt youth in tel aviv did it in that very stall
There are a lot of LGBTQ+ people here in HaKfar HaYarok, including me. There's a reason it says ”Here I loved my first love” in the lyrics of HaKfars hymn.
I fell in love with you. You were my roommate. I miss you so much.
Came out to my friends for the first time here. Years have passed and contact was lost, but man, I miss them.
told my dad i'm going out with another girl, it was very casual and so was his reaction. eighth grade
First kiss with a girl
First kiss with a girl
met the most beautiful girl i've ever seen in my life in theatre class.
came out here to my mom

I was closeted when I went to my first pride. I was there with IGY, and even though I knew I would be accepted I was afraid to come out. At pride, there were so many people. When I came back a year later, I was with my parents. This year, I'll be there with my friends.
I was scared I’d be alone here but I found my place with lgbt and straight people who accepted me
Got lost with my first girlfriend here
saw two women kissing on the bus for the first time (we were on the way to the pride parade)
We kissed here for the first time. You tasted like peach. I'm so glad for everything we had and also for where we are today.

you are not alone. אוהבת אתכם מאוד

(stay safe)
We kissed here for the first time. We joked about who's supposed to make the first move and you tasted like peach. I'm so glad for everything we had.

saw a movie here and realized that I'm bisexual

I was on my way home after a long day and saw someone on the street with trans and pan pride pins on their backpack. Desperately wanted to find a subtle way to show pan solidarity but all i ended up doing was cross the street. It made my day
I can't wait to come to Tel Aviv pride when I go to Israel for the first time. Being Jewish has helped me with understanding my queer identity. I love Eretz Yisrael.

I had to leave after they found out

Joined the queer alliance in the campus. First time I got to talk to someone who is like me in real life.

Laughing about Star Wars, being my himbo self and meeting you for the first time, I fell in love head over heels. I realised I was gay only several weeks before that, at age 30, and I was so happy I knew who I was. I'll probs never confess, but you are my pack and I love you, in more ways that I can count. I hope one day smth will happen, but even if not, its ok. I love you anyway, no matter what
On Shnat I asked my friends to use the pronouns that make me comfortable and happy. They did. Thank you.

you are the greatest love ive ever had and known. i’m so proud of you for coming out to your mom. even though i’m not out yet and it’s not so certain if this will ever work out for us i’m happy we have each other. i love you my beautiful angel. you make me so so happy every day i see you. i love driving back at 4am from kiryat-ono just so i could be with you for a couple of hours. so worth it.

i’ve know i’m bi since 8th grade. being in a religious community while dealing with my sexuality can be rough. if anyone else from modiin feels the same way as i do. drop a pin down below :) just want to know i’m not alone.

This was the spot where I met other queers for the first time in my life. It was 2001. I was barely out of the closet and scared my parents would never accept me. These people were my family for a long time. Some of them have since gone out of my life, but I still think fondly of them and hope they're doing OK, wherever they are <3

I came out to you and you asked me if it's ok for a young person to contact me in the future for advice on the matter. I didn't expect it, and I hope the young person is doing alright since they never contacted me.

Came out to my mom here after we went together to a queer rights protest. We sat at a coffee shop next to three drug queens who were also protesting. I said there was something I needed to tell her, but was so nervous and couldn't get the words out of my mouth. She asked if something bad had happened to me, if somebody hurt me. When I finally told her I was bi she was so relieved nothing bad happened that she laughed.
FREE PALESTINE

finally accepted im bi after a girl in our base took me aside for a talk where she asked me if i was really bi, said she "used to be bi herself", pinned me to her bed while on top of me in a platonic way, and i got so flustered i couldnt stop thinking about it 2 years later. israeli girls are the devil

i fell in love with her, here. she never knew.
her windy hair at sunset.. the first time I thought to myself "maybe I'm queer"
i hated this school but at least i met you.

this school sucks but at least I met you. I don't even care that you have a boyfriend, sometimes a one sided relationship is so strong its enough for both. I love you, but not in the way you think, not anymore. you forced me to grow up and I'll always be grateful.

Going to sem next year (2022). Really nervous because I will be forced into being Jewish, more than I want to be. I want to get a girlfriend here, my first one ever. Or at least have other queer people on Neve campus with me.

to my younger self & my current self: you are worthy of love, freedom, the sea, delicious food, amazing sex, flowers, your ancestors' guidance and blessings.
you will spend so many years wishing you could come home, but eventually you'll realize that home was in your heart the whole time.
never compromise on any part of who you are.

My grandparents were born here before their exodus in 1948, so please don’t support the occupation because of their pink-washing. I deserved to live here just as they did, I deserved to be indigenous to my land and not a stranger everywhere I went. I miss a city I’ve never seen and it hurts. A gay Palestinian is indigenous to here and always will be.

I’ve always imagined you and me sitting out in the sun, hand and hand, free at last. We spoke of all the places we would go if we could. Yet you are gone now. If I had known that bombs raining down on us would take you from me, I would have gladly told the world how I adored you more than anything. I’m sorry I was a coward.

Hi to anyone who might see this. I just wanna say that it gets better. It really does.

Be you, it'll be fine.
free palestine we love the gay palestinians

I dream of a time when Palestine is liberated and I can finally set foot in a place where my culture is not used as an excuse to oppress and enslave. Then and only then will I put my forehead to the Western Wall and cry a thousand tears for the members of my family who could not do the same
My religious bestie finally came out to me after waiting for ages for her to realize she’s bi and accept it.
My first kiss, which was already really bad, but then the (out of the closet gay) guy told me that he's not attracted to me and started having a sexuality crisis as I'm on his lap.

you made me get attached to you then left

He’s one of the only medically transitioning ppl (the only that he knows of). He doesn’t identify as trans because he can’t. i love him as my sibling. love from israel free palestine
Palestinian queer & refugee  here , I originally come from this village, Lifta that was ethnically cleansed by zionists in the 1948

my first time visiting the city my grandparents were expelled from was emotional for me as a queer Palestinian, I couldn't hold my tears. Having to face queer westerns telling me they had fun visiting the city I am from, the city i am supposed to live and grow in, posting pictures on Instagram in a place where my people  were ethnically cleansed from. Even though I hold the Belgium citizenship since I was 23 , I never had the courage to go there because I couldn't accept the fact that people that don't deserve to be there are living in peace , the fact that I can only enter my land using a foreign passport. I am sure one day I will return and will be able to live in Jafa, a place where I feel I belong to. We will return , we shall return

I’m a queer palestinian. I originally come from here, where Eurovision was held in 2019,  located on top of the remains of of al-Manshiyya, the village my grandparents were ethnically cleansed from. The village was erased from existence during the nakba in 1948

Our Queer Palestinians voices is higher than the brutality of the occupation and higher than the oppression that our society puts upon us, The voices of freedom will win and Palestine will be free and so we will be.🇵🇸

My sitti died without ever seeing this city again. In 1948 she was sent into exile from her homeland, from Jaffa "the bride of the sea". Before she died, she told me about a woman she once loved here. After the Nakba, she never saw her again. She never even found out if she survived. She told me she still thinks about her. I still mourn the life my grandmother might have been able to live here, in this city, in her homeland. I still mourn the love she shared with this woman, only to be torn apart by 60 years of exile. This land was her birthright. This life was her birthright. They were stolen from her. This place will be liberated. I will return one day to a liberated Palestine. For her.
Been kicked out my house lmao

I love them, been together for almost a year now, I am back home for winter break and seeing how different I am from all of my friends, how all of them talk about their boyfriends and I cant share how much I love her hurts, they will judge me so hard, it will get to my parents and my life will be ruined. It sucks to sleep everyday thinking about the day I have to choose my partner over my parents. Its also harder to always have to ask myself if what I am doing is the right thing. The only think im sure of is that our love is so pure and it doesn't happen twice in life. Im just so lost
Free Palestine.

During the Palestinian nakba, my grandparents were forced out of their land to Lebanon. Later on, my family immigrated to France during the Lebanese civil war. The only thing grandpa got from the house before fleeing was the key and a picture of him and grandma infront their house. He would always talk about jaffa oranges , his house and the Mediterranean sea. I grew up wanting to know how I am, where I’m originally from so in 2017 I decided to search from my grandparents original house in old Jaffa. Long story short, with the help of Palestinians living there we found the stairs that used to lead to my grandfather's house , we found the house . We found the lighted window which was once a kitchen window. As a queer Palestinian,  the only time I felt angry and broken about seeing a pride flag was when I saw it flying on grandparents house, on my stolen land.

we are palestinian refugees , some of us are queer. somewhere here a Palestinian village called Kafr 'Ana used to exist. i visited it yesterday and i wanted to document it on this website to remind my fellow queer family around the world that israel is a settler colony. i finally went to visit the place i hesitated for a very long time. non of my family members wanted to come , they told me they cant bare the pain standing on a place that was once a village. on my way to Kafr 'Ana i let the driver stop next to a house with oranges & a big israeli flag. i picked up some oranges as fast as I could before the owner of the house sees me. ' Its okay to do so , you're in your land ' said the driver. After we arrived to  Kafr 'Ana, i did something special , i picked up a bag and collected sand to my dad. he will definitely be happy. after that i went and touched the Mediterranean sea , the sea that was the biggest witness to our catastrophe.

A gay trans dude happened to be born here. My family left the country when I was a toddler. We came back for a visit in 2014, when I was a teen, arriving a few days before the Gaza War started. I remember hearing sirens, going into bomb shelters, and nothing happening to me or anyone else. Looking down at my phone and see crowds in Gaza standing next to bombed buildings, people hurt and killed. Later, I saw a tank on the highway - just a tiny taste of something I couldn't begin to imagine. Hoping Palestine can be free and united soon.
